summary: Set 056 Session 2 (2 of 3) - Validate the version-walk migration. S2 is the independent validation checkpoint for the documentation-authority migration committed out of band in e5a3476 and ratified by the S1 audit; it is validation-only and edited no document (the fixes were moved to Session 3 when the set grew 2 -> 3). Confirmed the canonical docs/repository-reference.md -> 'Documentation authority and release status' section is present and well-formed (principle + consumer table + release-status table + recent walk) with version claims accurate against package metadata (router 0.15.0, extension 0.27.0). Markdown render check clean; no broken-anchor risk (the engine-file Shared repo facts pointers reference the section by prose title and no live doc uses the #documentation-authority-and-release-status anchor as a link). Straggler grep sweep over live docs found TWO live stragglers S1's narrower grep missed: Finding A (HARD) docs/repository-reference.md:475 CLAUDE.md file-map row says shared facts 'live in this doc' contradicting the migration and inconsistent with the sibling AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md rows; Finding B (SOFT) CONTRIBUTING.md:9 cites CLAUDE.md for 'the consumer-repo map'. Both recorded with prescribed fixes and handed to Session 3. Authoritative record: s2-validation.md. Cross-provider verification by gemini-2.5-pro returned raw ISSUES_FOUND with one critical claiming a missed consumer-table straggler (the ## Consumer repos table duplicated in all three engine files); dispositioned in-flight as a context-gap FALSE POSITIVE against the locked S1 contract (s1-audit-record.md 3.3 explicitly permits a duplicated consumer table; the canonical copy verifiably exists in repository-reference.md so it is not sole-sourced; the verifier was not fed s1-audit-record.md), and re-cast as an explicit keep-vs-remove DECISION for Session 3 rather than dismissed. Both claim checks that matter held true, both S2 stragglers were independently confirmed real, and the verifier endorsed S2's deferral discipline as sound. Net S2 verdict VERIFIED_WITH_NOTES. No code, no release.

reason.specifics: Session 3 is the substantive complete-centralization + close pass: fix the two S2-recorded stragglers (repository-reference.md:475 file-map row; CONTRIBUTING.md:9 consumer-map citation), make the explicit keep-vs-remove decision on the engine-file consumer tables, symmetrize CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md to thin bootstrap + identical pointers (relocating any inline-only shared detail such as the e2e harness layers and router-config-editor walkthrough to engine-agnostic docs), then re-grep + structural-diff + cross-provider verify + close the set. Same engine keeps the audit/validation context continuous; high effort because this is the real centralization work, not a thin pass.
